status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 2 / CRITICAL PRODUCT IDENTITY BLOCK ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Chemically unresolved trademark/family-name cosmetic peptide product
mechanism_summary: Not assigned until the catalogue identifies whether the product is palmitoyl pentapeptide-4, Matrixyl 3000, Matrixyl Synthe'6 or another formulation.
evidence_maturity: Evidence is product- and INCI-specific; no single Matrixyl evidence base can be assigned to an unresolved catalogue powder.
regulatory_headline: COSMETIC FAMILY NAME / CHEMICAL IDENTITY UNRESOLVED — NO MEDICINAL CLAIM
closure_class: TRADEMARK FAMILY, INCI AND FORMULATION IDENTITY
closure_state: BLOCKED — MATRIXYL TRADEMARK VARIANT, INCI, PEPTIDE COMPONENTS AND FINISHED-FORM IDENTITY UNKNOWN
authoritative_product_boundary: Matrixyl is a trademark family, not one invariant molecule. Official Croda records distinguish original Matrixyl containing palmitoyl pentapeptide-4, Matrixyl 3000 containing palmitoyl tripeptide-1 plus palmitoyl tetrapeptide-7, and Matrixyl synthe'6 containing palmitoyl tripeptide-38. The MA10 powder cannot be assigned to any one variant without direct manufacturer and analytical evidence.
critical_closure_controls: No silent assignment to palmitoyl pentapeptide-4
No silent assignment to Matrixyl synthe'6
No transfer of finished cosmetic-formulation evidence to MA10 powder
No injectable, dosing or reconstitution interpretation
